---
title: "Design criteria and optimisation choices"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Design criteria and optimisation choices}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(LibeRality)
```

LibeRality groups criteria by the decision they answer.

| Question | Criteria |
|---|---|
| Estimate the full parameter vector | D, A, E |
| Estimate selected parameters or contrasts | Ds, c, L, RSE, prediction variance |
| Remain useful under uncertainty | Bayesian, robust, minimax, maximin, model average, precision probability |
| Distinguish models | T, KL, weighted model discrimination |
| Support a trial hypothesis | power, superiority, non-inferiority |
| Support a dosing or endpoint decision | target attainment, correct-dose probability, expected utility |
| Respect feasibility | cost, participant burden, and explicit constraints |
| Balance objectives | normalised compound criterion or Pareto frontier |

Bayesian and robust criteria average over the declared scenario distribution.
Minimax/maximin retain the worst scenario in the correct optimisation direction.
Prior information can be added as `prior_fim`; its row names are aligned to the
estimated parameter names.

## Choosing an optimiser

| Design space | Suggested method |
|---|---|
| Smooth continuous variables | L-BFGS-B |
| Small non-smooth continuous problem | Nelder-Mead |
| Sampling times or a finite grid | coordinate exchange |
| Mixed continuous, integer, discrete, or categorical | hybrid |
| Difficult global problem | particle swarm (`pso`) |
| Allocation weights | multiplicative or Fedorov-Wynn |
| Several objectives without agreed utility | Pareto exploration |

Constraints are evaluated independently of the scalar criterion and violations
receive a configurable quadratic penalty during optimisation. Statistical
constraints include maximum RSE, minimum power, and target/exposure attainment;
operational constraints include sample separation, samples per subject, total
subjects, allocation, duration, blood volume, and cost. Custom constraint
functions are suitable for local trusted runs but are intentionally rejected by
the typed remote-job wire format.

Theoretical optimality is only one layer of design qualification. Examine
matrix rank and conditioning, parameter/scenario sensitivity, optimiser
stability across seeds, exact integer rounding, protocol feasibility, and
empirical complete-trial operating characteristics before selecting a design.
